observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Inventory Committee
created pursuant to Executive Order No. 4, s. 2023, immediately prepare
and implement a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) in accordance with the
guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 to facilitate the
complete and accurate physical inventory and one-time cleansing of the
PPE account balances.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Accounting
and Supply Office to prepare and maintain updated Ledger and Property
cards, respectively, to be reconciled periodically.

We recommended that management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and systematic
monitoring, verification, and validation of all fund transfers to
barangays.
b. Furthermore, the Municipality promptly issue demand letters to
barangays with unliquidated balances and require the timely
submission of complete fund utilization reports to enforce
accountability and ensure transparency in the use of public funds.

We recommended and Management agreed that ensure the execution of
duly signed Memoranda of Agreement (MOAs) with recipient barangays
prior to the release of funds, in strict compliance with Item 3.1 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. The Municipal Treasurer require the Revenue Collection Clerks
(RCCs) to prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD)
and remit all collections in full to the Municipal Treasurer at the
close of each business day. For collectors assigned to field
stations where travel to the Treasurer’s Office exceeds one day,
remittances should be made at least once a week or immediately
once collections reach ₱5,000.00, whichever comes first.
b. The Municipal Treasurer strictly verify all collections and
accountable forms turned over by the collectors. All collections,
including those personally received and those remitted by the
RCCs, must be deposited intact with the authorized depository
bank on the same day or not later than the next banking day, in
compliance with Section 32 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly monitor the
expiration dates of fidelity bonds for accountable officers and ensure that
renewals are completed at least one month before the expiration date.

We recommended that Management submit the following documents to
the Office of the Auditor:
a. List of all ongoing Projects, Programs, and Activities (PPAs) as of
December 31, 2024;
b. Report of Publicized Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) as of
December 31, 2024;
c. First, Second, Third, and Fourth Quarter Reports for CY 2024 on
the utilization of the 20% Development Fund, including
information on unimplemented projects; and
d. Henceforth, strictly comply with the prescribed submission
requirements for PPAs and Development Fund utilization reports
to ensure transparency, facilitate audit procedures, and support the
effective delivery of municipal development initiatives.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Bids and Awards
Committee:
• Rate the Single Largest Completed Contract submitted by bidders as
“passed” only if it is supported by an Owner’s Certificate of Final
Acceptance issued by the project owner other than the contractor or a
final rating of at least Satisfactory in the Constructors Performance
Evaluation System in accordance with Section 23.4.2 of the IRR of RA
No. 9184; and
• Submit a written justification for the noted lapses in the preliminary
examination of bids.

We recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, the BAC
require the contractor to submit a DOLE approved Construction Safety and
Health Program prior to the contract signing to advocate the security and
safety of employed workers in the contract.

We recommended that Management:
a. Terminate the contract for the Improvement of Local Roads
(Package 4-2018) in accordance with the Guidelines on
Termination of Contracts under Annex “I” of the 2016 Revised
Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 9184;
b. Consider blacklisting the contractor, if warranted, due to a
negative slippage of 15% or more within the project's critical path,
attributable solely to the contractor’s fault or negligence, pursuant
to Appendix 17 – Uniform Guidelines for Blacklisting under the
2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
c. Direct the Project Engineer in charge to explain the failure to
recommend timely contract termination despite evident
contractor deficiencies, and to ensure strict compliance with
applicable procurement rules and contract implementation
guidelines in future projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Require the contractor concerned to immediately rectify the 27
square meters affected by moderate scaling in the above-
mentioned projects to conform with the approved Program of
Works, or pursue all possible legal remedies;
b) Conduct periodic monitoring of the 13.50 square meters affected
by minor scaling;
c) Provide complete documentation such as as-built plans, field
notes, and variation orders (if any) for the project “Concreting of
Local Roads Package 2-2022 at Poblacion East, Poblacion West,
Flora, Apayao”; otherwise, the related costs may be subject to
disallowance in audit;
d) Direct the Municipal Inspectorate Team and the Project-in-Charge
to henceforth conduct regular and strict monitoring and
supervision to ensure that all contractors implement projects in
accordance with the approved Program of Works.

We recommended management to:
a) Direct the Agency Engineer and the Inspectorate Team to
immediately evaluate the project site and coordinate with the
contractor to complete the unaccomplished works;
b) Submit a Variation Order, along with proper justification, for the
change in technical specification regarding the distance between
fence columns from the original 6 meters to 4 meters; and
c) Instruct the Project Engineer in charge of the project
"Construction of Perimeter Fence of the Slaughterhouse" to
compute the cost underrun resulting from the unimplemented 4-
meter portion and initiate the process for refund from the
contractor.

observations.recommendation:
1.9. We recommended and management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accounting Office to prepare the necessary
adjusting entries to set up the liability account for the 15 per cent
and record the July-December 2023 5 per cent collections from
building fees and other charges;
b. The Municipal Treasury Office and Municipal Accounting Office
prepare a separate account for the 15 per cent share of building
permit fees and other charges, and maintain a separate Trust
Fund or Trust Liability account;
c. The Building Official prepare a Program of Work detailing the
manner of utilization of the 15 per cent share to be held under
trust, to be approved by the Local Chief Executive, and to be
submitted to the DPWH Secretary thru the NCBDO;
d. The Municipal Treasury Office remits the 5 per cent DPWH Share; and
e. Henceforth, comply with the provisions of Presidential Decree
(PD) No. 1096 and DILG and DPWH Joint Memorandum Circular
(JMC) No. 001 relative to the accounting and remittance of
building permit fee collections.

2.5. We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a. The management to demands the immediate submission of the
liquidation document as proof that the funds were used for its
purpose. Otherwise, demand refunds of the cash advances from
the former employees; and
b. Henceforth comply with the specific provisions of EO No. 77 for
grant and liquidations of travel expenses to avoid long overdue
accounts.

3.7. We recommended that:
a. Management to conduct a physical count/inventory of all its PPE,
and prepares the prescribed reports in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
b. Consider the availment of the remedy of the one-time cleansing
of PPE accounts provided under the same COA Circular.

4.6. We recommended that Management optimize the utilization of its
LDRRMF to lessen the socio-economic and environmental impacts of
disasters in the community and disaster risks, and to enhance disaster
preparedness and response capabilities of the community by providing
appropriate programs/projects.

5.7. We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a. Management submit the Issuance Forms for In-Kind Donations –
DRRM/(distribution list) and monthly report on the receipt and
distribution/utilization/issuance of in-kind donations of donated
relief goods to facilitate audit/evaluation on the distribution of
donated relief goods to the beneficiaries; and
b. Henceforth, comply with the documentary requirement on the
distribution of donated goods as prescribed under pertinent
provision of COA Circular No. 2014-002.

6.9. We recommended that the:
a. Liquidating officer must be provided with a safety vault to secure
funds in her possession awaiting Deposit;
b. Collectors always secure the acknowledgment/signature of the
LO on their Reports of Collections and Deposits (RCDs) and
indicate the actual cash remittances on the space provided. The
Liquidating Officer (LO) shall receive and sign all RCDs and
verify the actual cash remittances received with what was
indicated by the collector in the RCDs;
c. Liquidating officer be proficient in recording the collection
remitted and deposits in the LO Cash book to avoid errors and
unrecorded remittances and for easier monitoring;
d. Liquidating Officer strictly adheres to Section 28 of COA Circular
No. 92-382 to deposit intact all his collections, as well as all
collections turned over to him by the collectors/tellers, with
the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next
banking day or at least once a week, or as soon as the
collections reach P10,000.00; and
e. OIC Municipal Treasurer strictly monitors the remittances of
collections by the collector and deposits made by the
Liquidating Officer to avoid accumulation of material amounts
for a considerable period in the possession of any accountable
officer.

8.8. We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. the Municipal Accountant to evaluates and reconcile prior years'
documents/records and data, to identify the nature and list of the
unremitted taxes withheld;

(RR) No. 11-2018, RR No. 2-2022, and RR No. 6-2001 in remitting
taxes withheld within the prescribed period to avoid the penalties
prescribed in the National Internal Revenue Code of 1997

observations.recommendation:
1.6. We recommended and the management agreed that:

a. the Pharmacy Unit furnish the accounting section with copies of
the Property Transfer Reports on COVID-19 Vaccines received
and returned from and to the Provincial Health Office of Apayao
together with the copies of the report on the utilization for her
basis to record the adjustments;

b. the Municipal Accountant records the following proposed
adjusting journal entries to recognize the unutilized vaccines
(Inventory End) in the books of accounts supported by the PTRs
and Utilization Report from the Pharmacy Unit; and
Drugs and Medicine for Distribution 613,328.19
Prior Period Adjustment 613,328.19

c. Management verifies or asks the amount of the received
vaccines with no indicated amount for proper valuation;
otherwise, a memorandum entry must be maintained for record
purposes by the accounting unit.

2.7. We recommended that the:

a. Revenue Collectors strictly adhere to Section 26 of COA Circular
No. 92-382 to turn over all of their collections intact to the
treasurer/cashier at the close of the business day or at least
once a week, or as soon as the collections reach P5,000.00;

b. OIC Municipal Treasurer, not to issue additional accountable
forms to the collectors unless collections pertaining to the
accountable forms previously issued to them are remitted intact;

c. Liquidating Officer strictly adheres to Section 28 of COA Circular
No. 92-382 to deposit intact all his collections, as well as all
collections turned over to him by the collectors/tellers, with
the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next
banking day, or at least once a week, or as soon as the
collections reach P10,000.00;

d. Avoid incurrence of over deposits by proper maintenance and
monitoring records and accounting of collections, personal
monies, and other funds of the LGU; and

e. Stop the practice of accepting/encashment/accommodation of
checks other than payment to the Municipality.

3.7. We recommended and management agreed that:

a. The Head of the Agency creates an Inventory Committee in
accordance with the required compositions as provided under
item 5.2 of COA circular 2020-006; and

b. Management shall conduct a physical count of all its PPE,
whether acquired through purchase or donation, including those
constructed by administration and found at the station and
prepares the prescribed reports in accordance with the
Procedural Guidelines stated on COA Circular 2020-006.

4.5. We recommended that the management:

a. Fast-track the implementation of the Programs/Projects and
Activities under the 20% Development Fund as programmed to
avoid the accumulation of unimplemented projects annually, in
order to address the priority needs of the constituents and to
help achieve and deliver desirable socio-economic and
environmental development and sustainability; and

b. Ensure that the development projects funded out of the 20% DF
are well-planned as to procurement and implementation
timetables.

5.18. We recommended that management provide a written
explanation/justification for the following in order to make a
decision in audit:

a. Why management only issued the first communications
on the noted defects of the project on April 17, 2018,
which is more than four (4) months after the lapse of the
contract period which is December 2, 2017;

b. Why the termination of the contract was done only on
August 10, 2022, which is more than four (4) years after
the issuance of the last notice. The 3rd notice was issued
on June 29, 2018, wherein the LGU has enough time to
terminate the contract prior to the expiration of the
performance bond which is July 18, 2018 in order to forfeit
it;

c. Why the termination of the contract was not done earlier
to be able to take over the projects and negotiate/award to
other technically, legally, and financially capable
contractors if management has no capabilities to conduct
it by administration; so that the remaining fund of
₱977,523.54 was used to complete the project instead of
reverting it to the national treasury due to long period of
Spill-over; and

d. Why management does not impose other appropriate
legal action against the defaulted contractor such as, but
not limited to blacklisting to avoid the same problem that
may cause by the contractor to other government
instrumentalities.

6.7. We recommended and management agreed that:

a. Locate and recover the missing properties to be included in the
properties for disposal. Otherwise, investigate who took them or
identify whose fault causes the loss for appropriate actions; and

b. Dispose of the unserviceable properties in accordance with
appropriate guidelines. Observing the following procedure such
as but not limited to:

b.1. The Officer Accountable shall return the Unserviceable
Property to the OIC-Municipal Treasurer (who also acts as
the General Services Officer (GSO)) with the use of Property
Return Slip and request for the cancellation of the
corresponding Memorandum Receipt of Property/Property
Acknowledgement Receipt;

b.2. The GSO shall prepare an Inventory and Inspection
Report of Unserviceable Property and submit it to the
Committee on Appraisal/Disposal for appropriate action;

b.3. The Committee on Appraisal/Disposal, upon the receipt of
the Inventory and Inspection Report inspect and appraise
the unserviceable property, submit the same to the
Municipal Auditor and request for the disposal thereof;
and

b.4. Upon recommendation of the Municipal Auditor, cause the
disposal and request that the same be dropped from the
books of accounts.

7.11. We recommended that:

a. Management submit a justification for why they opted to
negotiate with trading businesses/sub-dealers from far places
rather than to negotiate with direct/authorized dealers within the
nearby provinces;

b. Secure the copy of the original Certificate of Registration (CR) as
proof of ownership of the said vehicle and to avoid problems that
may occur due to possible loss; and

c. Management thru the Municipal Treasurer facilitates the transfer
of Vehicles’ Registration in the Municipality’s name in
compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 4136 for
proper monitoring and security of the said public vehicles.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. submit schedules to support balances of the accounts in the financial
statements and maintain subsidiary ledgers for all real and nominal
accounts to facilitate verification;
b. prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements pursuant to
Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011; and
c. coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to establish and properly
document transactions pertaining to Cash-Local Treasury and Petty Cash
Funds.

2.8 We recommended that Management thoroughly review prior years’
transactions and require the Municipal Treasurer to account for the missing
fund of ₱78,981.85.

2.9 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant record
transactions correctly to avoid misstatement in the financial statements that
could mislead its users.

3.14 We recommended that Management require:
a. the liquidating officer to perform her functions faithfully with respect
to the turnover and accounting for collections; demand from the
collectors the production of any unaccounted collections, otherwise,
she shall be held solidarily accountable for losses that may occur
through misappropriation, theft, robbery, or due to fortuitous events;
summarize all collections turned-over to her together with her own
collections; prepare the corresponding RCD; and deposit the same
intact to the bank; and
b. demand immediate restitution by sub-collectors of their cash
shortages, otherwise charges for malversation of public funds shall be
filed against them with the corresponding administrative action.

4.8 We recommended that Management through the Municipal Accountant:
a. stop granting cash advances to elective officials except for travels,
likewise, to un- bonded officials and employees, and to those with
outstanding cash advances; and
b. require that cash advances be properly supported with complete
documentation before processing payment.

5.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant correct the entry made by
crediting Prior Years Adjustment for the collections on the sale of bidding
documents and debiting Other Payables for ₱144,000.00.

6.5 We recommended that Management:
a. require grantees to submit certified copy of identifications (IDs) to
permit verification of the signatures on the payroll;
b. require submission of authorization letter for the assistance received by
persons other than the payee; and
c. strictly follow the guidelines and/or revisit the implementing guidelines
on the grant of educational assistance to avoid undue discretion on the
grant of the same.

7.4 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Municipal
Accountant, the duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names of the
taxpayers and the tax due at the start of each year, as basis of recording in
the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables in order to present reliable
and accurate receivable and deferred income account balances as well as to
determine tax collection efficiency and effectiveness of the municipal
government.

8.6 We recommended that Management promptly submit copies of perfected
contracts in accordance with Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001
to allow the Audit Team ample time for review and evaluation.
